target_case.question: In what year were the pioneers of managed care in the United States established?
target_case.summary_1:
Evidence found:
- Group Health Cooperative — [1]: "Group Health Cooperative was one of the pioneers of managed care in the United States;"
- Medic One — [1]: "establishment of Medic One in 1970."

Ambiguities:
- "pioneers of managed care" — plural but only "Group Health Cooperative" is named in [1].
- "Group Health Cooperative" — passages give no establishment year or variant labels.

Bridge / missing fact:
- The establishment/founding year of "Group Health Cooperative" is not provided in the passages.

Retrieval direction:
- Fetch page "Group Health Cooperative" (Wikipedia page title).
- Search query: "Group Health Cooperative founded" or '"Group Health Cooperative" "established"'.
- Alternate: "Group Health (Washington) founding year" or "Group Health Cooperative of Puget Sound founded year".
Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets below.
target_case.summary_2: Group Health Cooperative was established in 1945.

delta_p.why_local_transition: Resolves the specific ambiguity of phrases like 'romantic comedy-drama' by standardizing to the hyphenated core genre token so the final-answer contract (one minimal string) can be satisfied using only the available summaries.

delta_p.output_contract: Produce exactly one field `answer` whose value is a single minimal string (no extra text, punctuation, or markup). After normalization above, emit that core company name as the sole answer token.
delta_p.why_local_transition: This is a minimal formatting/normalization change: it removes corporate suffixes and parenthetical abbreviations from an otherwise-correct company name so the final output is the concise core name expected by the endpoint rules, while preserving evidence selection and other base behaviors.
